notes: All classes share the same MoveSpeedBase = 300 and MaxMoveSpeed cap = 330.
Speed formula (reconstructed): FinalSpeed = clamp(MoveSpeedBase + MoveSpeedAdd, 0, MaxMoveSpeed) * (1 + MoveSpeedMod/100) * activeMovementMultipliers.
MoveSpeedArmorPenalty is a separate UI-tracking attribute that records the cumulative armor speed cost without being consumed by the final formula directly.
MovementModifiers (multipliers) are applied stacked — e.g., Crouch + BackwardMove = 0.65 * 0.8 = 0.52x.
Haste MoveSpeedMod=50 interpreted as +50 speed modifier units. The formula meaning (+50% vs +50 flat) is encoded in blueprint logic and not directly readable from data assets.
Sprint adds +10 flat MoveSpeedAdd (not a percentage), only to Fighter and GrandMaster Fighter.
Armor items set MoveSpeed via GE_ItemPropertyMoveSpeed, which writes to both MoveSpeedBase (the primary additive channel) and MoveSpeedArmorPenalty (the UI tracking channel) simultaneously.
ValueRatio = 0.001 is used for MoveSpeedBonus item properties, converting stored integer values to float multipliers (e.g., stored 1000 = 1.0 = 100%).

baseMovementSpeeds._note: All player classes initialize with the same MoveSpeedBase. Per-class max caps are all 330. Source: PlayerCharacterEffect files + Constant files.

movementModifiers._note: DCMovementModifierDataAsset multipliers. Applied multiplicatively on top of the computed speed. Multiply=0.0 means immobile. JumpZMultiply affects jump height. Multiple modifiers stack multiplicatively.

Crouch.note: Player-activated crouch. Reduces speed to 65% of current.

Walk.note: Toggled walk mode (hold-walk key). Very slow but quiet movement.

Airborne.note: Airborne state. No horizontal control; near-zero gravity scale override.

Levitation.note: Levitation spell state. Player cannot horizontally move; reduced gravity.

Landed._note: Id_MovementModifier_Landed exists but Multiply not explicitly set — no landing penalty.

Wet.note: Being wet reduces jump height to 70% but does NOT slow horizontal movement.

StickyTar.note: Tar flask / sticky tar area. Severe 65% speed reduction.

SwimSprint.note: Speed boost while sprinting in water.

BoatBackwardMove.note: Slower than regular backward movement while on boat.

actionStates._note: Modifiers applied during weapon actions (attacks, blocks, casting, equipping). These stack with stance modifiers. All have JumpZMultiply=0.

Equip.note: Drawing or equipping a weapon. 10% speed penalty.

Wear.note: Putting on armor. Matches crouch speed — significant penalty during equip animation.

sprintSystem._note: Sprint is NOT a MovementModifier multiplier. It is a timed DCGameplayEffectDataAsset that adds flat MoveSpeedAdd. Source: Id_ActorStatusEffect_Sprint.json + Id_Skill_Sprint.json.
sprintSystem.effectiveBoost: +10 units on top of current MoveSpeedBase (300 base = ~3.3% relative boost)
sprintSystem.note: Sprint is an active skill, not a passive sprint toggle. Available exclusively to Fighter. Applies a flat +10 MoveSpeedAdd for 2 seconds with a 28-second cooldown.

crouchSpeedPenalty.note: Crouch reduces speed to 65%. At base 300: 300 * 0.65 = 195 units. Stacks multiplicatively with backward movement: 0.65 * 0.8 = 0.52x (156 units at base).

armorWeightSystem._note: Armor items apply a MoveSpeed penalty directly to the DCAttributeSet.MoveSpeedBase attribute via GE_ItemPropertyMoveSpeed. A parallel DCAttributeSet.MoveSpeedArmorPenalty attribute tracks the cumulative penalty for UI display. There is NO tiered encumbrance system or weight class found in raw data.
armorWeightSystem.mechanism: Per-item flat value applied via GE_ItemPropertyMoveSpeed → additive on MoveSpeedBase + parallel write to MoveSpeedArmorPenalty
armorWeightSystem.analysis: Because MoveSpeedArmorPenalty is written separately from (but equal to) the item's MoveSpeedBase delta, the UI can display total armor speed cost without needing to subtract a base value. The actual movement speed uses only MoveSpeedBase in the final computation.

notableStatusEffects._note: Speed-affecting actor status effects. MoveSpeedMod values are applied to the DCAttributeSet.MoveSpeedMod attribute.

Haste.note: Major speed buff. MoveSpeedMod+50 + full action speed boost. 6 second duration.

Sprint.note: Fighter exclusive. See sprintSystem for full details.

Kinesthesia_Perk.note: Passive perk that provides a persistent 15% movement multiplier when active.

attributeSchema._note: Attributes in DCAttributeSet relevant to movement. All are additive-stacked from gameplay effects.
attributeSchema.MoveSpeedBase: Core movement speed. Initialized to class base (300). Modified additively by items (armor = negative, boots = positive) and other effects.
attributeSchema.MoveSpeedMod: Percentage modifier. Stacks additively across sources. Applied as multiplier in final formula.
attributeSchema.MoveSpeedAdd: Flat speed bonus. Added after base calculation. Used by Sprint (+10) and enchanted items.
attributeSchema.MoveSpeedArmorPenalty: UI-tracking attribute mirroring the cumulative armor speed penalty. Not part of the primary movement formula — exists so the HUD can report 'penalty from armor' independently.
attributeSchema.MaxMoveSpeed: Per-class speed cap constant (330 for all classes). Final computed speed is clamped to this.

formulaAnalysis.reconstructedFormula: FinalSpeed = clamp( (MoveSpeedBase + MoveSpeedAdd) * (1 + MoveSpeedMod / 100), 0, MaxMoveSpeed ) * product(activeMovementModifier.Multiply)
caveats: Exact formula order is in Blueprint C++ logic (GA_Sprint, GE_Haste) — not directly readable from data assets.
MoveSpeedMod semantic (percent vs flat) is inferred from context: ValueRatio=0.001 on items and Haste value=50 suggest it is a percentage stored as integer.
Whether MaxMoveSpeed cap is applied before or after MovementModifier multipliers is unknown from data alone.
MoveSpeedArmorPenalty may or may not be additively included in FinalSpeed; evidence suggests it is a shadow attribute for UI.

exampleCalculation.scenario: Rogue base, wearing BloodwovenRobe (-5 MoveSpeed), crouching, moving backward
